Rhythmic aims to generalize stablecoin technology for the mass market by partnering with consumer-facing companies to embed financial products like cashback and rewards into their existing brand ecosystems. The company plans to launch midyear, enabling brands to offer co-branded Visa cards and manage user balances and rewards with stablecoins powering the backend infrastructure.

title: American Express and Visa alumni raise $4 million for Rhythmic, which partners with brands to provide financial products for their customers

Visa+ also integrates with partners like DailyPay, i2c, TabaPay, and Western Union to expand its reach to use cases including gig economy payouts, with a general availability planned for mid-2024.

title: From SBI, HDFC to ICICI Bank: Why credit card issuers are tightening their reward policies
summary: Credit card companies like American Express, ICICI Bank, SBI, and HDFC Bank are tightening their reward policies, making it harder for users to earn benefits. This shift involves moving to more demanding milestone structures, reducing perks, and increasing spending requirements for rewards like lounge access and bonus points. Banks are adjusting these policies due to rising defaults and the high cost of benefits, prompting cardholders to strategize their spending to optimize card usage.

Payment networks like Visa and Mastercard are increasingly supporting crypto-linked cards, moving them from niche products to integral parts of the global payment landscape. House of Doge aims to integrate Dogecoin into existing payment systems, lowering development costs and accelerating market entry to enable seamless global usage.

title: Global Crypto Card Spending Surges to $18 Billion Annually as House of Doge Targets Integration of Dogecoin Into Traditional Payment Rails
summary: Global crypto card spending has reached an annualized run rate of $18 billion, signifying a major shift towards real-world crypto usage. House of Doge, the corporate arm of the Dogecoin Foundation, aims to integrate Dogecoin into traditional payment systems, positioning it as a seamless option for everyday transactions alongside traditional cards and mobile payments. This integration is facilitated by partnerships with major global payment networks, accelerating adoption and moving crypto from speculation to utility.

title: It’s Still Card-First in the U.S.
summary: New research from Global Payments Inc. indicates that credit and debit cards continue to dominate U.S. online and in-store shopping, accounting for 49% and 71% respectively in 2025. Despite this shift, physical cards remain the favored funding source within digital wallets, suggesting continued relevance for card networks.

title: Why Your Favorite Company’s Economic Moat May Not Be Built to Last
summary: Pat Dorsey, founder of Dorsey Asset Management and former director of equity research for Morningstar, discusses the concept of economic moats in companies. Dorsey also cautions against overestimating the durability of network effects as a moat source, citing PayPal as an example.

The exchange requires participants to sign a Makewhole Agreement, which entails uncapped payment obligations tied to future adjustments in the Applicable Conversion Rates related to ongoing U.S. covered litigation. If fully subscribed, this offer could lead to the issuance of approximately 61.38 million Class B-3 shares and 24.07 million Class C shares.
